problem_statement.title: Soft deprecate literal data without I()
problem_statement.issue_title: readr literal data tickles a lifecycle warning emitted by vroom
problem_statement.issue_body:
Warning Message Received, concerning a deprecated vroom() argument likely used in readr package: 

```r
Warning:
The `file` argument of `vroom()` must use `I()` for literal data as of vroom 1.5.0.
  
  # Bad:
  vroom("X,Y\n1.5,2.3\n")
  
  # Good:
  vroom(I("X,Y\n1.5,2.3\n"))
ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the readr package.
  Please report the issue at <https://github.com/tidyverse/readr/issues>.
This warning is displayed once per session.
Call lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings() to see where this warning was generated. 

> lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()

[[1]]
<warning/lifecycle_warning_deprecated>
Warning:
The `file` argument of `vroom()` must use `I()` for literal data as of vroom 1.5.0.
  
  # Bad:
  vroom("X,Y\n1.5,2.3\n")
  
  # Good:
  vroom(I("X,Y\n1.5,2.3\n"))
ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the readr package.
  Please report the issue at <https://github.com/tidyverse/readr/issues>.
---
Backtrace:
     ▆
  1. ├─base::try(...)
  2. │ └─base::tryCatch(...)
  3. │   └─base (local) tryCatchList(expr, classes, parentenv, handlers)
  4. │     └─base (local) tryCatchOne(expr, names, parentenv, handlers[[1L]])
  5. │       └─base (local) doTryCatch(return(expr), name, parentenv, handler)
  6. ├─... %>% bind_rows()
  7. ├─dplyr::bind_rows(.)
  8. │ └─rlang::list2(...)
  9. ├─base::lapply(...)
 10. │ └─global FUN(X[[i]], ...)
 11. │   └─global get_cubes(cube = x, codes = codes, polite_sleep_secs = 0.3)
 12. │     └─purrr (local) safe_gen_cube(cube, database = "genesis")
 13. │       ├─base::tryCatch(...)
 14. │       │ └─base (local) tryCatchList(expr, classes, parentenv, handlers)
 15. │       │   └─base (local) tryCatchOne(expr, names, parentenv, handlers[[1L]])
 16. │       │     └─base (local) doTryCatch(return(expr), name, parentenv, handler)
 17. │       └─global .f(...)
 18. │         └─restatis::gen_cube(...)
 19. │           └─restatis:::gen_cube_(name, ...)
 20. │             └─cube_raw %>% read_cube() %>% rename_cube_data_columns()
 21. ├─restatis:::rename_cube_data_columns(.)
 22. ├─restatis:::read_cube(.)
 23. │ └─resp %>% httr2::resp_body_string() %>% readr::read_lines()
 24. └─readr::read_lines(.)
 25.   └─vroom::vroom_lines(...)
 26.     └─vroom:::standardise_path(file)
```

patch:
diff --git a/NEWS.md b/NEWS.md
index aded0a9e..eb389b01 100644
--- a/NEWS.md
+++ b/NEWS.md
@@ -1,5 +1,7 @@
 # readr (development version)
 
+* `read_csv()`, `read_csv2()`, `read_tsv()`, `read_delim()`, and `read_fwf()` now emit a deprecation warning when literal data is passed without wrapping it in `I()`. For example, usage like `read_csv(I("x,y\n1,2"))` is preferred over `read_csv("x,y\n1,2")` (#1611).
+
 * `read_table2()` has been removed from readr. This function was deprecated in
   readr 2.0.0 (2021-07-20) in favor of `read_table()`. Use `read_table()`
   instead.
diff --git a/R/read_delim.R b/R/read_delim.R
index e76462b1..7e44170b 100644
--- a/R/read_delim.R
+++ b/R/read_delim.R
@@ -267,6 +267,13 @@ read_delim <- function(
     )
   }
 
+  file <- standardise_literal_data(
+    file,
+    "read_delim",
+    env = current_env(),
+    user_env = caller_env()
+  )
+
   vroom::vroom(
     file,
     delim = delim,
@@ -359,6 +366,14 @@ read_csv <- function(
       )
     )
   }
+
+  file <- standardise_literal_data(
+    file,
+    "read_csv",
+    env = current_env(),
+    user_env = caller_env()
+  )
+
   vroom::vroom(
     file,
     delim = ",",
@@ -457,6 +472,14 @@ read_csv2 <- function(
       )
     )
   }
+
+  file <- standardise_literal_data(
+    file,
+    "read_csv2",
+    env = current_env(),
+    user_env = caller_env()
+  )
+
   vroom::vroom(
     file,
     delim = ";",
@@ -548,6 +571,13 @@ read_tsv <- function(
     )
   }
 
+  file <- standardise_literal_data(
+    file,
+    "read_tsv",
+    env = current_env(),
+    user_env = caller_env()
+  )
+
   vroom::vroom(
     file,
     delim = "\t",
@@ -575,6 +605,40 @@ read_tsv <- function(
 }
 
 # Helper functions for reading from delimited files ----------------------------
+
+# This became necessary when vroom 1.7.0 moved up to deprecate_warn() for
+# literal data that was not wrapped in I().
+#
+# We don't want readr users:
+# * To go abruptly from no message to deprecate_warn()
+# * To get a message from vroom that tells them to open an issue on readr
+standardise_literal_data <- function(file, fn, env, user_env) {
+  if (
+    is.character(file) &&
+      length(file) == 1 &&
+      grepl("\n", file) &&
+      !inherits(file, "AsIs")
+  ) {
+    lifecycle::deprecate_soft(
+      "2.2.0",
+      paste0(fn, "(file = 'should use `I()` for literal data')"),
+      details = c(
+        " " = "",
+        " " = "# Bad (for example):",
+        " " = 'read_csv("x,y\\n1,2")',
+        " " = "",
+        " " = "# Good:",
+        " " = 'read_csv(I("x,y\\n1,2"))'
+      ),
+      env = env,
+      user_env = user_env
+    )
+    I(file)
+  } else {
+    file
+  }
+}
+
 read_tokens <- function(
   data,
   tokenizer,
diff --git a/R/read_fwf.R b/R/read_fwf.R
index a22bb907..5f9da631 100644
--- a/R/read_fwf.R
+++ b/R/read_fwf.R
@@ -143,6 +143,13 @@ read_fwf <- function(
     return(warn_problems(out))
   }
 
+  file <- standardise_literal_data(
+    file,
+    "read_fwf",
+    env = current_env(),
+    user_env = caller_env()
+  )
+
   vroom::vroom_fwf(
     file,
     col_positions = col_positions,

+++ b/R/source.R

-#'    containing at least one new line, or be a vector containing at least one
-#'    string with a new line.
+#'    literal data, wrap the input with `I()`.

 #'    Using a value of [clipboard()] will read from the system clipboard.
 #'

 Using a value of \code{\link[=clipboard]{clipboard()}} will read from the system clipboard.}

diff --git a/tests/testthat/_snaps/read-csv.md b/tests/testthat/_snaps/read-csv.md
index 4d1041d6..2d0b71cc 100644
--- a/tests/testthat/_snaps/read-csv.md
+++ b/tests/testthat/_snaps/read-csv.md
@@ -6,3 +6,61 @@
       Error:
       ! `delim` must be at least one character, use `read_table()` for whitespace delimited input.
 
+# literal data without I() emits deprecation warning (#1611)
+
+    Code
+      x <- read_csv("x,y\n1,2", show_col_types = FALSE)
+    Condition <lifecycle_warning_deprecated>
+      Warning:
+      The `file` argument of `read_csv()` should use `I()` for literal data as of readr 2.2.0.
+        
+        # Bad (for example):
+        read_csv("x,y\n1,2")
+        
+        # Good:
+        read_csv(I("x,y\n1,2"))
+
+---
+
+    Code
+      x <- read_csv2("x;y\n1;2", show_col_types = FALSE)
+    Message <cliMessage>
+      i Using "','" as decimal and "'.'" as grouping mark. Use `read_delim()` for more control.
+    Condition <lifecycle_warning_deprecated>
+      Warning:
+      The `file` argument of `read_csv2()` should use `I()` for literal data as of readr 2.2.0.
+        
+        # Bad (for example):
+        read_csv("x,y\n1,2")
+        
+        # Good:
+        read_csv(I("x,y\n1,2"))
+
+---
+
+    Code
+      x <- read_tsv("x\ty\n1\t2", show_col_types = FALSE)
+    Condition <lifecycle_warning_deprecated>
+      Warning:
+      The `file` argument of `read_tsv()` should use `I()` for literal data as of readr 2.2.0.
+        
+        # Bad (for example):
+        read_csv("x,y\n1,2")
+        
+        # Good:
+        read_csv(I("x,y\n1,2"))
+
+---
+
+    Code
+      x <- read_delim("x|y\n1|2", delim = "|", show_col_types = FALSE)
+    Condition <lifecycle_warning_deprecated>
+      Warning:
+      The `file` argument of `read_delim()` should use `I()` for literal data as of readr 2.2.0.
+        
+        # Bad (for example):
+        read_csv("x,y\n1,2")
+        
+        # Good:
+        read_csv(I("x,y\n1,2"))
+
diff --git a/tests/testthat/_snaps/read-fwf.md b/tests/testthat/_snaps/read-fwf.md
new file mode 100644
index 00000000..c78dd896
--- /dev/null
+++ b/tests/testthat/_snaps/read-fwf.md
@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+# literal data without I() emits deprecation warning (#1611)
+
+    Code
+      x <- read_fwf("abcd\nefgh", fwf_widths(c(2, 2)), show_col_types = FALSE)
+    Condition <lifecycle_warning_deprecated>
+      Warning:
+      The `file` argument of `read_fwf()` should use `I()` for literal data as of readr 2.2.0.
+        
+        # Bad (for example):
+        read_csv("x,y\n1,2")
+        
+        # Good:
+        read_csv(I("x,y\n1,2"))
+
diff --git a/tests/testthat/test-read-csv.R b/tests/testthat/test-read-csv.R
index aeaa5bc0..dad103b0 100644
--- a/tests/testthat/test-read-csv.R
+++ b/tests/testthat/test-read-csv.R
@@ -467,3 +467,51 @@ test_that("read_tsv correctly uses the quote and na arguments (#1254, #1255)", {
   expect_equal(x[[1]], c("\"one baz\"", "three"))
   expect_equal(x[[2]], c("two", ""))
 })
+
+test_that("literal data without I() emits deprecation warning (#1611)", {
+  skip_if_edition_first()
+  withr::local_options(lifecycle_verbosity = "warning")
+
+  expect_snapshot(
+    x <- read_csv("x,y\n1,2", show_col_types = FALSE),
+    cnd_class = TRUE
+  )
+  expect_snapshot(
+    x <- read_csv2("x;y\n1;2", show_col_types = FALSE),
+    cnd_class = TRUE
+  )
+  expect_snapshot(
+    x <- read_tsv("x\ty\n1\t2", show_col_types = FALSE),
+    cnd_class = TRUE
+  )
+  expect_snapshot(
+    x <- read_delim("x|y\n1|2", delim = "|", show_col_types = FALSE),
+    cnd_class = TRUE
+  )
+})
+
+test_that("literal data with I() does not warn", {
+  expect_no_condition(
+    read_csv(I("x,y\n1,2"), show_col_types = FALSE)
+  )
+  # read_csv2 emits an info message about decimal mark, so expect_message()
+  # absorbs that
+  expect_message(
+    expect_no_warning(
+      read_csv2(I("x;y\n1;2"), show_col_types = FALSE),
+      class = "lifecycle_warning_deprecated"
+    )
+  )
+  expect_no_condition(
+    read_tsv(I("x\ty\n1\t2"), show_col_types = FALSE)
+  )
+  expect_no_condition(
+    read_delim(I("x|y\n1|2"), delim = "|", show_col_types = FALSE)
+  )
+})
+
+test_that("file paths do not trigger literal data warning", {
+  expect_no_condition(
+    read_csv(test_fixture("basic-df.csv"), show_col_types = FALSE)
+  )
+})
diff --git a/tests/testthat/test-read-fwf.R b/tests/testthat/test-read-fwf.R
index 4397de6e..0eeffb5b 100644
--- a/tests/testthat/test-read-fwf.R
+++ b/tests/testthat/test-read-fwf.R
@@ -285,3 +285,19 @@ test_that("fwf_positions always returns col_names as character (#797)", {
   expect_type(info$end, "double")
   expect_type(info$col_names, "character")
 })
+
+test_that("literal data without I() emits deprecation warning (#1611)", {
+  skip_if_edition_first()
+  withr::local_options(lifecycle_verbosity = "warning")
+
+  expect_snapshot(
+    x <- read_fwf("abcd\nefgh", fwf_widths(c(2, 2)), show_col_types = FALSE),
+    cnd_class = TRUE
+  )
+})
+
+test_that("literal data with I() does not warn", {
+  expect_no_condition(
+    read_fwf(I("abcd\nefgh"), fwf_widths(c(2, 2)), show_col_types = FALSE)
+  )
+})

fail_to_pass: read_csv("x,y\n1,2") should emit a lifecycle deprecation warning about using I() for literal data
read_csv2("x;y\n1;2") should emit a lifecycle deprecation warning about using I() for literal data
read_tsv("x\ty\n1\t2") should emit a lifecycle deprecation warning about using I() for literal data
read_delim("x|y\n1|2", delim = "|") should emit a lifecycle deprecation warning about using I() for literal data
read_fwf("abcd\nefgh", fwf_widths(c(2, 2))) should emit a lifecycle deprecation warning about using I() for literal data
pass_to_pass: read_csv(I("x,y\n1,2")) should NOT emit any deprecation warning
read_csv2(I("x;y\n1;2")) should NOT emit a deprecation warning
read_tsv(I("x\ty\n1\t2")) should NOT emit any deprecation warning
read_delim(I("x|y\n1|2"), delim = "|") should NOT emit any deprecation warning
read_fwf(I("abcd\nefgh"), fwf_widths(c(2, 2))) should NOT emit any deprecation warning
read_csv(path_to_actual_file) should NOT emit a deprecation warning (file paths should be distinguished from literal data)

key_changes: Detect literal data: `is.character(file) && length(file) == 1 && grepl("\n", file) && !inherits(file, "AsIs")`
Use `lifecycle::deprecate_soft()` with version '2.2.0', function name, and helpful bad/good examples
Apply helper to read_delim, read_csv, read_csv2, read_tsv (in R/read_delim.R) and read_fwf (in R/read_fwf.R)
Update documentation to clarify that I() should be used for literal data
potential_pitfalls: Not checking for 'AsIs' class will cause double-wrapping or infinite warnings
Not distinguishing file paths from literal data - file paths should NOT trigger the warning
Using wrong environment parameters in deprecate_soft() will show incorrect source location
Forgetting to apply the helper to all relevant read functions
Not handling the case where file is a vector with multiple elements

instruction: Implement a soft deprecation warning for readr functions when literal data is passed without wrapping it in `I()`. The vroom package (a dependency) now requires `I()` for literal data, and readr needs to handle this gracefully by emitting its own soft deprecation warning and automatically wrapping the data in `I()`. Create a helper function `standardise_literal_data()` that detects literal data (single character strings containing newlines that aren't already wrapped in `I()`), emits a soft deprecation warning using `lifecycle::deprecate_soft()`, and wraps the data in `I()`. Apply this helper to `read_delim()`, `read_csv()`, `read_csv2()`, `read_tsv()`, and `read_fwf()`.
context:
The helper function should:
- Check if `file` is a single character string containing a newline (`\n`)
- Check that it doesn't already inherit from class 'AsIs' (which `I()` adds)
- Use `lifecycle::deprecate_soft()` with appropriate version, function name, and helpful examples
- Return `I(file)` for literal data, otherwise return `file` unchanged

Function signature pattern:
r
standardise_literal_data <- function(file, fn, env, user_env) {
  # detection logic here
  lifecycle::deprecate_soft(...)
  I(file)
}


Call pattern in read functions:
r
file <- standardise_literal_data(
  file,
  "read_csv",
  env = current_env(),
  user_env = caller_env()
)
